Monkey gets the ice-cream and then she
What happens after Monkey climbs the tree?
options: Monkey falls out of the tree.

questions.text: What does the word SPLAT! tell you about the drip?
options: the place where the drip lands

questions.text: Why does Monkey breathe out?
options: The other animals have gone away.
She has eaten all the ice-cream.
She has started to feel ill.

... they contain hardly any fat or salt.
The text says this to show that bananas
What does the text tell you about banana skin?
options: It can be used in salads.
It is a good source of fibre.

questions.text: In the text, what does Versatile mean?
Bananas do not need to be stored in the refrigerator. This piece of information belongs under the heading
The main purpose of the text is to tell you
options: how to make a banana smoothie.
when is the best time to buy bananas.

questions.text: This story is about a man who
options: wants his cats to help him.

questions.text: Why does Leon make the backpacks?
options: for the cats to sit in
for people to put letters in
to give to the children in the village

questions.text: Why does Leon think his plan will work?
options: People will chase the cats away.
The cats like meeting new people.
People hate using the red postboxes.
The cats will return home for dinner.

questions.text: What is one thing Leon finds out about his cats?
options: They can walk a long way.
They do not like being outside.

questions.text: What idea does Leon have at the end of the story?
options: to use the dog as his helper
to make the dog scare away the cats

questions.text: Which words best describe Leon?
The purpose of the text is to show
options: how honey bees talk to each other.

questions.text: What do the arrows show in the text?
options: which picture belongs with each box
the order to read the information
which piece of information is the most important
the direction that honey bees move around the hive

questions.text: A honey bee uses its tongue like
How do honey bees turn nectar into honey?
options: by adding beeswax to the nectar
by storing the nectar in their nectar sacs
by reducing the amount of water in the nectar
by passing nectar between the honey stomach and real stomach

questions.text: Soon, young honey bees will be flying around looking for nectar ... The way this sentence finishes ( ... ) suggests that
options: the honey will stay fresh for a long time.
the honey is not good enough to eat.
the life cycle of honey bees continues.
the honey bees have a happy life.

questions.text: What does the text suggest about honey bees?
options: They make a lot of noise.
They get along with other insects.

questions.text:
Write the numbers 1 to 5 in the boxes to show the order of events in the text. The first one (1) has been done for you.

[ ] A honey bee chews the nectar.
[1] A honey bee collects the nectar.
[ ] A honey bee uses its wings to dry the nectar.
[ ] A honey bee adds beeswax to the honeycomb.
[ ] A honey bee passes the nectar to another honey bee.
T-Rex is included in the poem to show that butterflies
options: have lived for a long time.

questions.text: In this poem the small thing is
The poet admires the butterfly because it
options: helps to create food for others.

questions.text: Which word gives the main idea of this poem?
Why are the two questions asked in this poem?
options: to teach the reader the facts
to make the reader give answers
to encourage the reader to wonder
to check the reader understands what is happening

options: The Djaru people look after the area.
There is an underground river system.
The tourists bring water into the crater.
Rain gathers in the middle of the crater.

questions.text: According to the text, what did the rainbow serpent do?
options: It filled the crater with sand.
It came up through the crater.
It slithered around outside the crater.

options: to explain how to get to Kandimalal
to encourage tourists to visit Kandimalal
to help protect the environment around Kandimalal
